sections.heading: Sector 1
sections.body:
The lap begins with a long straight and DRS wide open. Brake just before the 100m board, down to 2nd gear. This is a tight 90-degree left-hander — stay close to the wall on entry and clip the inside kerb slightly. Dont run wide on exit; theres a wall waiting for you. This is a prime overtaking zone, so be prepared to defend and attack frequently.

Another 90-degree left with a short burst after Turn 1. Brake at about the 75m mark, again down to 2nd gear. Use minimal kerb on entry, and focus on getting a good exit — this leads into the first major DRS zone. Traction is key here, so short-shift if necessary.

Use DRS and hug the wall on the left. You will be at full throttle for a while, so this is a good spot to manage ERS deployment or set up for an overtake.

Another right-angle left-hander. Brake at the 100m board, down to 2nd gear. Stay tight to the wall on the left and avoid mounting the inside kerb too much — it can destabilise the rear of the car. Prioritise exit for the next short run to Turn 4.

Same story as Turn 3— another 90-degree left. Brake lightly, stay in 2nd gear, and keep it neat. The wall comes quickly on the outside if you drift wide.

sections.heading: Sector 2
sections.body:
A quick left-right chicane. Light braking and taken in 3rd gear. These are deceptively tricky: avoid over-rotating on entry and watch your exit line — the wall narrows quickly. Keep your steering inputs as smooth as possible.

Next up is the tight left into the narrowest section on the calendar. Brake early and rotate the car completely before entry. Stay off the inside kerb; hitting it even slightly can send you into the wall. Use 1st or 2nd gear, depending on your comfort with the cars rotation and setup.

This is a super tight uphill left-right-left sequence. Keep it in 2nd gear, minimal throttle, and guide the car through. Do not rush. Hug the kerbs, but dont touch; launch your car over them. Contact through this section will end your lap, or even worse, your entire race.

Next up is the fast left-right chicane, youre climbing and building speed. These can be taken in 4th or 5th gear with confidence. Use the road but watch for overcorrection; the walls stay close.

Tight right-handers where you must brake early and rotate fully. Turn 15 is especially dangerous — its downhill and off-camber. Brake around 80m, down to 2nd gear, and avoid carrying too much speed in. Its easy to understeer into the outside wall here.

sections.heading: Sector 3
sections.body:
A key left-hander that sets you up for the very long final straight. Brake at about 100m, down to 3rd gear. Hug the inside kerb and aim for a quick, clean exit. This corner directly affects your top speed down the main straight, so dont compromise it.

These are technically corners, but all flat-out. Keep DRS open (once enabled), avoid scraping the wall, and stay tight on the racing line to minimise distance.

This is the longest straight in Formula One. Keep the car stable and ERS deployed if youre pushing hard in qualifying or chasing down a rival. This is where the majority of overtaking will take place. Stay in the slipstream and make sure to pay attention to the braking board at Turn 1.
